title: A Quiet Place: Day One Opening Weekend Box Office
text: A Quiet Place: Day One opened with $53 million domestically, surpassing industry expectations that had projected closer to $40 million. The Paramount prequel achieved the franchise's strongest debut weekend. The film outperformed tracking estimates in its competition with Inside Out 2, which earned $57.4 million in its third week. This marked a rare occurrence the last time two films grossed over $50 million simultaneously was July 2023 when Barbie and Oppenheimer opened. The prequel's success challenged recent industry concerns about the viability of prequel releases. Made for $67 million, it benefited from premium summer placement and elevated ticket prices, despite a challenging year for horror films overall. The film received a B+ CinemaScore with a 68 Metacritic score, suggesting moderate audience reception. Its per-theater average reached $14,293 across 3,707 locations outperforming Kevin Costner's Horizon: An American Saga, which averaged only $2,683 per theater.
